compartment.title: Plan 1 reviewed twice by Momus/Metis: 9 issues found and fixed, declared ready for execution
compartment.p1:
User asked to review `.omo/plans/autodev-pi-foundation-1-core.md` and confirm readiness with Momus (Plan Critic) and Metis (Plan Consultant). Both reviewers independently found the same root cause: the plan referenced source files that don't exist.
**Round 1 — 7 issues found (commits 46b2ae5):** - CRITICAL: Identity source broken — plan said extract from `.opencode/oh-my-openagent.json` but `.opencode/` doesn't exist. Actual agent definitions are in `src-agents/` (hyphen, not `src/agents/`), with 13 `.yaml` files containing a `narrative:` field that IS the identity text. - CRITICAL: Explore agent has no source files — must be authored from scratch using AGENTS.md/ARCHITECTURE.md as spec. - CRITICAL: Module count contradicted across 3 places (12 vs 14+ vs 9). ARCHITECTURE.md was source of truth. - HIGH: T5 overloaded (skeleton + 9 modules + tests in one todo). Split into Phase 1 (skeleton + stubs) + Phase 2 (4 foundation modules with real logic). - HIGH: T4 said "validate model strings with test API call" but acceptance criteria said "no real API call during development." Changed to static allowlist at `.autodev/config/models.json`. - MEDIUM: Dependency matrix incomplete — T5 depends on T3+T4 (was just T4). T6 needs shared mock fixture. - MEDIUM: Stale references — docs-corpus count 218→119, `src/agents/`→`src-agents/`, file counts updated.
Agent verified actual repo state: `src-agents/` has 26 files (13 .md + 13 .yaml), all 13 YAMLs have `narrative:` fields. All 7 fixes applied and committed.
A system continuation directive fired claiming 0/7 tasks complete; agent verified all 7 were already done in commit 46b2ae5 and updated the todo list.
**Round 2 — 2 new blockers + 3 improvements:** - Module count "16" claimed but only 15 enumerated in actual lists (both ARCHITECTURE.md and T5). Changed to "15" everywhere (6 locations in plan + 1 in ARCHITECTURE.md). - ARCHITECTURE.md line 115 still referenced stale identity sources ("old OpenCode config", `.autodev/agents/*.yaml`). Rewritten to reference `src-agents/*.yaml`. - `models.json` creation moved from T4 to T3 (T3 owns config files). - `test/` and `test/mocks/` directory creation made explicit in T6. - T5 Phase 2 per-module sub-checklist added (team-mode: 12 tools, comment-checker: 1 handler, intent-gate: 2 hooks, notepad: 5 mappings).
Both Momus and Metis confirmed all fixes resolved. Plan 1 declared READY FOR EXECUTION.
compartment.p2: User requested Momus + Metis review of Plan 1 before execution. Round 1 found 7 issues (commit 46b2ae5): identity source was `src-agents/*.yaml` `narrative:` field (not `.opencode/oh-my-openagent.json` which doesn't exist), Explore agent must be authored from scratch, module count reconciled to 15, T5 split into skeleton+stubs and 4 foundation modules, model validation via static allowlist not API calls, dependency matrix fixed, stale references updated. Round 2 found 2 more: module count "16"→"15" (only 15 enumerated), ARCHITECTURE.md still had stale identity refs. All 9 fixed. Plan 1 declared ready.
compartment.p3: Two rounds of Momus/Metis review on Plan 1 found 9 issues total. Key fix: identity source is `src-agents/*.yaml` `narrative:` field (not the nonexistent `.opencode/oh-my-openagent.json`). Explore agent must be authored from scratch. Module count reconciled to 15. T5 split into skeleton + 4 foundation modules. Model validation via static allowlist. Plan 1 declared ready for execution.
compartment.p4: Plan 1 Momus/Metis review; src-agents/*.yaml narrative field is identity source; Explore authored from scratch; 15 modules; commit 46b2ae5

compartment.title: Plans 2, 3, 4 reviewed for consistency: SOURCE OF TRUTH blocks, mock strategy, stale references fixed
compartment.p1:
U: autodev-pi-foundation-1-core implementation has begun. while that is in progress, review autodev-pi-foundation-2-engine.md, and autodev-pi-foundation-3-knowledge.md. Ensure all the latest design decisions are incorporated, all features and modules contain clear implementation instructions, and everything is in compliance with all source of truth documentation.
Agent reviewed Plans 2 and 3 while Plan 1 was executing. Plan 2 had 5 issues: missing SOURCE OF TRUTH block, Must Have listed only 5 hard stops (missing `never-deploy-directly`), T9 said "validate against provider API" (contradicts static allowlist), wrong TypeBox import path (`@sinclair/typebox`→`typebox`), no mock strategy guidance. Plan 3 had 5 issues: missing SOURCE OF TRUTH block, docs-corpus count "218" in 4 locations (actual 119), T12 confusingly listed search_lore/search_docs as tools to register (those are T10/T11), no mock strategy, success criteria said "218 files." All fixed and committed.
U: yes, review plan 4
Plan 4 was in better shape — already had 7 design decisions. 5 issues found: missing SOURCE OF TRUTH block, missing Mock Strategy section (XL plan with 7 todos), Design Spec table said "34 sections" (should be 35 after module reconciliation), T13 "Blocked by" incomplete (only T7-T9, should be T7-T12), T19 missing real-verification note. All fixed and committed.
All 4 sub-plans now have consistent structure: SOURCE OF TRUTH block, Mock Strategy section, correct design decisions, correct section count.
compartment.p2: User asked to review Plans 2+3 while Plan 1 executed, then Plan 4. Plan 2: added SOURCE OF TRUTH, fixed hard stop count (5→6), static allowlist, TypeBox import, mock strategy. Plan 3: added SOURCE OF TRUTH, fixed docs-corpus count (218→119), clarified T12 scope, mock strategy. Plan 4: added SOURCE OF TRUTH, mock strategy, section count (34→35), T13 deps, T19 real-verification note. All 4 plans now have consistent structure.
compartment.p3: Plans 2, 3, 4 reviewed for consistency with Plan 1 and source-of-truth docs. All got SOURCE OF TRUTH blocks, Mock Strategy sections, and corrected stale references (218→119 files, 34→35 sections, TypeBox import, hard stop count).
compartment.p4: Plans 2-4 consistency review; SOURCE OF TRUTH blocks added; mock strategy sections; docs-corpus 119 files; 35 sections

compartment.title: Cross-plan Momus/Metis deep alignment review: 12 systemic issues found and fixed after conflict recovery
compartment.p1:
U: review plan 2, 3 and 4 with metis and momus. ensure alignment with all documentation as well as plan 1
Both reviewers found significant cross-plan issues. 12 total:
1. 5 integration modules orphaned (both reviewers) — Plan 1 T5 says lsp, tmux, mcp-integrations, rules-injection, watch-officer-monitor get real logic in Plan 4, but Plan 4 had zero todos for any. Fix: added T20 to Plan 4 covering all 5. 2. IntentGate missing from T13 dispatch (Metis) — ARCHITECTURE.md mandates IntentGate analyzes issue text before Cynefin classification. Fix: added IntentGate pre-processing step to T13. 3. "one-task-at-a-time" guardrail has no state backend (Metis) — T7 needs active-task counter but state backends (T13 work-items, T16 boulder.json) don't exist in Wave 2. Fix: T7 creates `.autodev/active-task.json` itself ({task_id, started_at}). 4. T5 notepad forward dependency on T10 (Metis) — Plan 1 T5 notepad stores decisions via Loreguard, but Loreguard is built in Plan 3 T10. Fix: notepad falls back to ctx_memory until Loreguard available; checks if search_lore tool exists. 5. Boulder state path inconsistency (Metis) — ARCHITECTURE.md said `.omo/boulder.json` reads plans from `.autodev/plans/` but plans are in `.omo/plans/`. Fix: changed to `.omo/plans/` in ARCHITECTURE.md and Plan 4 T16. 6. Debate session count 5 vs 6 (Metis) — Phase 3 cross-examination uses a shared session = 6 total, not 5. Fix: updated ARCHITECTURE.md and Plan 4 T15 to "5 independent + 1 shared." 7. Stale section references (Momus) — Plan 2 T7 referenced "section 4.1", Plan 4 T13 referenced "section 2.3" and "section 3.1" — none exist. Fix: replaced with correct section names (Guardrail Engine, Labels, Debate). 8. ARCHITECTURE.md says "218 files" (Momus) — actual 119. Fix: changed to 119. 9. "follow-the-plan" guardrail detection undefined (Metis) — no mechanism specified. Fix: SOFT STOP (warn) with heuristic file-scope check comparing write/edit targets against plan scope. 10. Loreguard ratification actor undefined (Metis) — who ratifies? Fix: NEMO ONLY — ratify_lore checks calling agent. 11. `docs_rebuild` missing from T19 installer (Metis) — search_docs returns empty until manually rebuilt. Fix: added step (h) `autodev docs rebuild` to T19. 12. Concurrency vs one-task-at-a-time conflict (Metis) — T13 said "process issues concurrently" but T7 said "one-task-at-a-time." Fix: triage concurrent, implementation serialized.
U: I'm sorry - due to my mistake there was conflicting changes made to the files you were working on. review the current state of plan files and architecture.md and restart your fixes
First attempt's edits got lost due to conflicting file changes. Agent re-read current state of all files and re-applied all 12 fixes with strict per-edit verification. All 12 verified and committed.
compartment.p2: Momus/Metis deep cross-plan review found 12 systemic issues: 5 orphaned integration modules (added T20 to Plan 4), IntentGate missing from dispatch, one-task-at-a-time had no state backend (T7 creates `.autodev/active-task.json`), notepad forward dep on Loreguard (falls back to ctx_memory), boulder path `.autodev/plans/`→`.omo/plans/`, debate 5→6 sessions, stale section refs, ARCHITECTURE.md 218→119 files, follow-the-plan detection undefined, ratification actor undefined (Nemo only), docs_rebuild missing from installer, concurrency vs one-task conflict. First fix attempt lost to conflicting changes; user told agent to restart. All 12 re-applied and committed.
compartment.p3: Cross-plan Momus/Metis review found 12 systemic issues across Plans 2-4 and ARCHITECTURE.md: orphaned modules, missing IntentGate in dispatch, state backend gaps, forward dependencies, path inconsistencies, session count, stale references, undefined detection/ratification mechanisms, missing installer step, concurrency conflict. All fixed after a conflict-recovery restart.
compartment.p4: cross-plan review; 12 issues; orphaned modules T20; IntentGate in dispatch; .autodev/active-task.json; notepad ctx_memory fallback; .omo/plans/ boulder path; debate 6 sessions

compartment.title: Plan 1 implementation verified: code works but agent-specific design notes missing from prompts
compartment.p1:
User reported Plan 1 implementation complete. Agent reviewed actual committed code against Plan 1 acceptance criteria.
**Verified working:** T1 (pi-foundation branch, 26 src-agents/ files), T3 (5 config files, deps installed, models.json with 2 valid strings), T4 (13 agents, correct model routing, Explore authored from scratch with 7 investigator matches), T5 (15 module dirs, 4 foundation modules 733 LOC, 11 stubs, package.json pi key), T6 (mock fixture, 88 tests pass/0 fail). Success criteria: 0 OpenCode imports, 0 OmO deps.
**HIGH issue found:** Agent-specific design decision notes from T4 notes (a)-(f) were NOT appended to agent prompts. The agents were ported from `src-agents/*.yaml` narratives but the design-decision-specific additions required by T4 were missing: (a) Harbor Master missing "sole user-facing point of contact", "all other agents invisible", "tracks active project", "permanent interface" — 0 matches; (b) Watch Officer missing "proactive monitoring", "plan deviations", "API mismatches", "team mailbox" — 0 matches; (e) Harbor Master missing liaison conditional — 0 matches; (f) Nemo GitHub PM — only 1 minimal match. Notes (c) Explore investigator and (d) Watch Officer engine-room role were present.
**MEDIUM issue:** No final wave evidence files at `.omo/evidence/` — F1/F2/F3 marked complete but not recorded.
The agent prompts are the core enforcement mechanism for 4 of the 7 design decisions. Without them, agents won't know about Harbor Master exclusivity, Watch Officer proactive monitoring, conditional liaison, or GitHub-as-sole-PM.
compartment.p2: Agent verified Plan 1 implementation against acceptance criteria. Code works: 88 tests pass, deps resolve, 15 module dirs, 13 agents ported, 0 OpenCode/OmO deps. But found HIGH issue: 4 of 7 design decisions not in agent prompts — Harbor Master sole contact (0 matches), Watch Officer proactive monitoring (0 matches), liaison conditional (0 matches), GitHub PM (1 minimal match). The agent-specific notes T4 required were not appended during porting. Also: no final wave evidence files recorded.
compartment.p3: Plan 1 implementation verified: code functionally complete (88 tests, deps resolve, 13 agents, 15 modules) but agent prompts missing 4 of 7 design-decision-specific notes (Harbor Master sole contact, Watch Officer proactive, liaison conditional, GitHub PM).
compartment.p4: Plan 1 verification; 88 tests pass; missing agent-specific design notes in prompts; Harbor Master sole contact; Watch Officer proactive

compartment.title: Plan 1 re-verified complete, Plan 2 reviewed by Momus/Metis: 12 fixes applied including provider-level concurrency
compartment.p1:
U: the fixes were implemented by another agent. Verify completion and recheck all implemented code against @.omo/plans/autodev-pi-foundation-1-core.md. Then review @.omo/plans/autodev-pi-foundation-2-engine.md and consult momus and metis to confirm it is ready for deployment. Do not make assumptions, check against actual implemented code.
Agent re-verified Plan 1: all 6 design notes now present (Harbor Master sole contact=3 matches, Watch Officer proactive=6, liaison conditional=2, GitHub PM=1, Explore investigator=6). Plan 1 fully complete.
Then consulted Momus and Metis on Plan 2. Found 3 must-fix + 9 recommended:
**Must-fix:** 1. `follow-the-plan` SOFT vs HARD contradiction — Plan line 41 says "6 hard stops", line 78 says "SOFT STOP (warn)", guardrails.yaml says `enforcement: block_action` (HARD). Fix: HARD stop everywhere. 2. Concurrency "key" undefined — "max 5 per key" but key = provider? model? agent? session? Tests won't catch wrong choice (mocks don't hit rate limits). Fix needed: specify key. 3. Evidence path conflict — Plan says `.omo/evidence/`, AGENTS.md Standing Order 5 says `.autodev/evidence/`. Fix: standardize on `.omo/evidence/`, flag AGENTS.md as stale.
U: explain issue 2
Agent explained 4 interpretations of "key": (1) provider — all sessions to same provider share 5 slots, entire crew capped at 5; (2) model — each model gets own 5-slot pool; (3) agent name — each agent type gets 5, no provider protection; (4) parent session — most granular, least protective. Agent recommended Interpretation 2 (model-level).
U: Interpretation 1 should be adopted. Implement this and the remaining fixes
User chose Interpretation 1 (provider-level) — more conservative, caps entire crew at 5 concurrent background tasks per provider. All 12 fixes applied and committed: concurrency key = provider with config at `.autodev/config/concurrency.yaml`; follow-the-plan changed to HARD stop; evidence path standardized on `.omo/evidence/`; expression evaluator specified (==, !=, >, AND, NOT, path_starts_with(), contains_secrets()); guardrail engine requires ALL 10 hard stops from YAML (not just 6); circuit breaker sliding window semantics; tests use 50ms timeout with fake timers; thinkingLevel is createAgentSession param not model string suffix; load_skills silently ignored with `// TODO(T12)`; shared pi-session mock at `test/mocks/pi-session.ts`; section count 34→35; stub replacement mechanics stated (replace register() body only, don't touch index.ts).
compartment.p2:
U: Do not make assumptions, check against actual implemented code.
Re-verified Plan 1 — all 6 design notes now in agent prompts. Then Momus/Metis reviewed Plan 2: 3 must-fix (follow-the-plan HARD vs SOFT contradiction, concurrency key undefined, evidence path conflict) + 9 recommended. User asked about concurrency key; agent explained 4 interpretations and recommended model-level. User chose provider-level (Interpretation 1): all sessions to same LLM provider share one 5-slot pool. All 12 fixes applied: concurrency config at `.autodev/config/concurrency.yaml`, follow-the-plan as HARD stop, 10 hard stops from guardrails.yaml, expression evaluator specified, circuit breaker sliding window, tests use 50ms fake timers, thinkingLevel clarification, shared mock fixture, section count fix.
compartment.p3: Plan 1 re-verified complete (all design notes in prompts). Plan 2 reviewed by Momus/Metis: 12 fixes applied. Key decision: concurrency key = LLM provider (user chose provider-level over agent's recommended model-level). follow-the-plan is HARD stop. Evidence path = `.omo/evidence/`. Guardrail engine loads all 10 hard stops from YAML.
compartment.p4: Plan 2 review; concurrency key=provider; follow-the-plan HARD stop; .autodev/config/concurrency.yaml; 10 hard stops; expression evaluator; sliding window circuit breaker

compartment.title: Plan 3 reviewed by Momus/Metis: 6 blocking issues found, ratify_lore design proposed, fixes pending
compartment.p1:
User asked to review Plan 3. Momus found only minor issues (stale stub comment, missing embeddings dir, 5-vs-4 skills count). Metis found 6 blocking issues:
1. **ratify_lore identity check unimplementable** (HIGH) — pi's ExtensionContext has no agent identity field. Cannot programmatically verify which agent is calling ratify_lore. 2. **FTS5 external-content table needs triggers** (HIGH) — without AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers, FTS table stays empty. 3. **90MB ONNX download in tests** (MEDIUM) — slow, CI-fragile. Need mock embedding fixture. 4. **session_search is NOT a SessionManager API** (MEDIUM) — T12 must implement the search loop itself (list → open → getEntries → text filter). 5. **autodev-onboard skill ambiguity** (MEDIUM) — 5 dirs but plan says "4 skills." Port as 5th skill or exclude as CLI-only? 6. **Notepad→suggest_lore wiring not captured** (MEDIUM) — T10 doesn't update T5's notepad to call suggest_lore().
Agent raised 3 questions to user via the question tool. User answered: (1) ratify_lore identity — use self-identification pattern; (2) autodev-onboard — port as skill; (3) ONNX — use VoyageAI as primary embedding provider with mock in tests.
Agent proposed ratify_lore design: tools are suggest_lore/ratify_lore/approve_lore/reject_lore/search_lore. Ratification requires 3 distinct approvers who self-identify via tool parameter (no pi API identity check). Auto-ratifies when 3 approvals recorded; rejections keep status at `under-review`. Category-based routing (fact/onboarding/design) at dispatch level. For design category, debate protocol (T15) handles adversarial review, output ratified via ratify_lore after debate.
U: proceed
Fixes not yet applied — work in progress at end of chunk.
compartment.p2: Plan 3 reviewed by Momus (minor) and Metis (6 blocking). Key issues: pi ExtensionContext has no agent identity field (ratify_lore can't verify caller), FTS5 needs triggers, ONNX in tests too slow, session_search not a SessionManager API, autodev-onboard skill ambiguity, notepad→suggest_lore wiring missing. User answered 3 design questions via question tool. Agent proposed ratify_lore design: 3 distinct self-identifying approvers, auto-ratify on 3 approvals, category routing at dispatch level. User said "proceed." Fixes pending.
compartment.p3: Plan 3 review found 6 blocking issues. Key discovery: pi ExtensionContext has no agent identity field — ratify_lore must use self-identification. Proposed design: 3 distinct approvers, auto-ratify on 3 approvals, debate handles design-category review. Fixes not yet applied.
compartment.p4: Plan 3 review; pi ExtensionContext no agent identity; ratify_lore self-identification; FTS5 triggers; 6 blocking issues; fixes pending

facts.PROJECT_RULES: * All plans must be reviewed by Momus (Plan Critic) and Metis (Plan Consultant) before execution approval. No plan executes without both reviewers' sign-off. * Verify implementation claims against actual committed code, not completion reports. Check agent files, module directories, test outputs, and grep results against plan acceptance criteria.
facts.ARCHITECTURE: * Background agent concurrency control is keyed by LLM provider string (e.g., `ollama-cloud`). All sessions to the same provider share one 5-slot pool (max 5 concurrent, configurable). Config at `.autodev/config/concurrency.yaml`. User chose provider-level over model-level to be more conservative. * `follow-the-plan` guardrail is a HARD stop (blocks action), not a SOFT stop (warn only). Consistent with guardrails.yaml, workflow-specification.md, and ARCHITECTURE.md. * Guardrail engine implements 10 hard stops via tool_call event interception, loaded from `.autodev/config/guardrails.yaml` (not hardcoded). The 6 named hard stops plus never-approve-own-work, never-modify-reference-docs, never-modify-debate-transcripts, and one additional. Expression evaluator supports ==, !=, >, AND, NOT, path_starts_with(), contains_secrets(). * Loreguard ratification requires 3 distinct approvers. Approver self-identifies via tool parameter (no pi API identity check). Auto-ratifies when 3 approvals recorded; rejections keep status at `under-review`. Category-based routing (fact/onboarding/design) happens at dispatch level, not tool level. For design category, the debate protocol (T15) handles adversarial review, output ratified via ratify_lore after debate concludes.
facts.CONSTRAINTS: * pi ExtensionContext has no agent identity field — tools cannot programmatically verify which agent is calling them. Approver self-identification via tool parameter is the workaround pattern.
facts.CONFIG_VALUES: * Circuit breaker stale timeout: 180s (configurable; tests use 50ms with fake timers). Sliding window semantics — time since last event, not session start. * One-task-at-a-time state backend: `.autodev/active-task.json` with {task_id, started_at}. Created by T7 itself, does not depend on T13 or T16. * Guardrail rules config: `.autodev/config/guardrails.yaml` — expression evaluator supports ==, !=, >, AND, NOT, path_starts_with(), contains_secrets().

causal_incident.summary: pi ExtensionContext has no agent identity field — ratify_lore cannot programmatically verify calling agent
causal_incident.symptom: Plan 3 T10 ratify_lore design assumed pi's ExtensionContext could identify the calling agent to enforce "Nemo only" ratification. Pi SDK exposes no agent identity field in tool context.
causal_incident.cause_summary: pi's ExtensionContext does not expose which agent is making a tool call — agent identity is not available to tool implementations
causal_incident.evidence: "ratify_lore identity check unimplementable — pi's ExtensionContext has no agent identity field"
causal_incident.fix_summary: Approver self-identifies via tool parameter; 3 distinct approver names required (enforced by storage layer); orchestration layer (T13 dispatch) ensures correct agents are assigned to review

output.user_observations: * User requires adversarial review (Momus + Metis) before approving any plan for execution — this is a consistent, non-negotiable workflow pattern. * User asks for detailed explanations of technical issues before making decisions, rather than rubber-stamping agent recommendations. * User chose the more conservative concurrency interpretation (provider-level) over the agent's recommended model-level approach.
